name: Multiple Sensitive Containers Stopped
notes:
This rule triggers when multiple sensitive containers are being stopped. This may indicate an intruder is compromising sensitive information.

Note: The Sensitive Container IDs reference set must be populated with the relevant Container IDs.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Machine Stop Success
when any of Container ID (custom) are contained in any of Sensitive Container IDs - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Container ID (custom) in 24 hour(s)
thresholds: when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Container ID (custom) in 24 hour(s)
TD_list: APPLY Multiple Sensitive Containers Stopped on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Machine Stop Success
AND when any of Container ID (custom) are contained in any of Sensitive Container IDs - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Container ID (custom) in 24 hour(s)
RED_list: Event name: Multiple Sensitive Container Stopped
Event description: Detects when multiple sensitive containers are stopped.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
